architecture.key_insight: Each feature is a fully self-contained module with its own item/, client/, data/, menu/, network/, recipe/ sub-packages. Modules share code through library/.

packages.tools: Complete tool system: item/, modifiers/, stats/, recipe/, menu/, network/, logic/, client/, data/
packages.smeltery: Smeltery multiblock system (similar internal structure to tools)
packages.library: Shared framework: materials/, modifiers/, module/, recipe/, utils/, json/, fluid/
packages.shared: Shared registrations and common code between modules

registration.pattern: Each module has its own registration constants. TinkerTools.java holds tool items, TinkerToolParts.java holds tool part items. Library provides shared registration utilities.

key_patterns.name: Self-Contained Feature Module
key_patterns.description: Each top-level package (tools, smeltery, gadgets) is a complete mini-mod with its own internal structure. This is the MOST scalable pattern for very large mods with 300+ classes.
key_patterns.internal_structure: item/ + client/ + data/ + menu/ + network/ + recipe/ + logic/

key_patterns.name: Library as Internal Framework
key_patterns.description: library/ provides shared base classes, utilities, and abstractions. All feature modules depend ON library, but NOT on each other. This enforces clean module boundaries.

key_patterns.name: Modifier System Design
key_patterns.description: Tinkers' modifier system is a composable behavior pattern. Each modifier is a separate class that can be applied to any tool. This is the most complex and elegant system in MC modding.
key_patterns.pattern: Modifier interface → concrete implementations → applied via tool stats → rendered via client/
